rows.notes: Very clear single year written at top centre of page. Ink and numerals legible; no amounts associated.
Title is clear in large script across the top centre. No monetary columns on this line.
Date line clearly reads '2do die Novembris.' in Latin-style script; legible though slightly stylised.
Year repeated below the date. Clear and unambiguous.

Monetary columns well aligned. Pounds '920' and shillings '0' are clear. Pence looks like '11' with a small '3/4' mark (interpreted as three farthings = 0.75). Description word 'Arravagia' is somewhat uncertain in spelling (likely 'arrearages'), hence reduced confidence.
Amounts clear and in valid ranges: 227 pounds, 10 shillings, 1 penny with a '3/4' fraction mark (0.75). Description 'Anni praesentis' is legible.
Pounds '2639' and shillings '1' clearly aligned. No fraction or pence visible (pence = 0). Description 'Varia' clear.

Single horizontal line above this amount indicates a subtotal. Number reads 3707 pounds, 0 shillings, 1 penny with a halfpenny sign (interpreted as 0.5). Column alignment used from neighboring rows; values in valid ranges.
Amounts read as 637.5.9 3/4 (3/4 = 0.75). Text 'Debita anni praecedentis' is legible though compact; fraction mark visible and interpreted as three farthings.
Clear summary line 'Summa Receptorum' with amount 4424 pounds, 5 shillings, 11 pence and a small quarter-farthings mark (appears as '1/4' = 0.25). Column rulers and alignment consistent.
Section header 'Soluta' (payments) clearly written on left margin, introduces following payment entries.

Amount columns clear: 673 pounds, 7 shillings, 10 pence with a small quarter-farthings mark (0.25). The description appears to read '1 Do Rectori. Schol: &c' (abbreviated Latin); the precise abbreviations are somewhat uncertain, lowering confidence.
Clear amounts 360 pounds, 12 shillings, 7 pence. Description 'ordinaria' readable. All numeric values fall within valid ranges.
Amounts read as 2305 pounds, 5 shillings, 11 pence with a '3/4' mark (0.75). Description 'Varia' is clear.
Subtotal for payments under a horizontal rule. Amount reads 3347 pounds, 6 shillings, 5 pence. Columns well aligned and numbers distinct.
Repeats the receipts total 'Ex Receptis' with identical amount to earlier Summa Receptorum: 4424.5.11 1/4 (1/4 interpreted as 0.25). Clear alignment and labelling.
Clear line 'Solutis Deductis' showing the payments total 3347.6.5. Matches earlier subtotal; numerals and placement unambiguous.
Final balance 'Restat solvendum' reads 1076 pounds, 19 shillings, 6 pence with a small '1/4' fraction (interpreted as 0.25). Column alignment and figures clear.
The verification statement is clearly written in the lower half of the page. No monetary values on this line.
First signature line reads clearly as 'J. C. Jones.' followed by an abbreviation 'Rectorum' (legible). No amounts. Slight flourish in handwriting but name is readable.

Second signature looks like 'Gul: Dalby' with an office abbreviation (sub-rectorem / subrectorum). Handwriting is smaller and somewhat compressed, so reading of surname and the abbreviation is moderately uncertain.
Signature appears to be 'Jos: Rogers' with an abbreviation for bursar (Burs: Sen:). Letters are readable but some ink thinning makes exact abbreviations moderately uncertain.

This signature is partly obscured / flourished. The beginning 'Gul. Ed.' is fairly clear, but the family name is difficult to decipher (rendered here as 'A***'). The post-nominal 'S.T.B.' (or similar) appears present. Low confidence due to ink flourish and partial obscuration.
Signature looks like 'R B Paul' followed by an abbreviation (possibly 'Burs. Jun.'), but letters are small and somewhat indistinct; moderate uncertainty in exact transcription.
Final signature reads 'J. L. Richards' with degree abbreviation 'A.M.' The handwriting is reasonably clear; minor flourish but readable.
